as_of.title: Brief Remarks on the Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy

inflation.label: broadening / underlying
inflation.note: Beyond Middle East energy and tariffs, core stuck at 3 percent and nonhousing services still elevated, with risk to long-term expectations.
inflation.quote: Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices and is a good guide to future inflation, likely was 3 percent in February, about where it was a year ago. Nonhousing services inflation has remained elevated.

labor.label: downside risks
labor.note: Job creation near zero for the past year in a 'low hire, low fire' environment, unusual outside a recession.
labor.quote: Job creation has also been close to zero for the past year, a highly unusual experience outside of a recession. We are in a "low hire, low fire" environment.

stance.label: appropriate / well positioned
stance.note: Supported the FOMC decision to maintain the current policy setting amid sticky inflation and a fragile labor market.
stance.quote: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) met last week and chose to maintain the current setting of monetary policy—a decision I supported.
